summary: Set 033 Session 5 of 6: shipped the queueing/polling feature from the spec. Net-new module src/providers/CheckoutPollService.ts (~310 lines) implements a directory-watch + prompt + 5s-debounced poll state machine over ~/.dabbler/checkout-conflicts/. The detection contract is a per-conflict JSON sentinel file (schemaVersion: 1) carrying the four spec-named surface fields (heldByEngine, heldByProvider, sessionSetPath, checkedOutAt) plus the would-be holder identity + model + effort + sessionNumber needed to retry start_session without re-resolving session-state.json. Both invokers emit on EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT (4): claude-session-start-invoker.js (Node subprocess) writes the sentinel before its existing stderr-log path; codex/configWatcher.ts (in-process) now captures the spawn's exit code via child.on('exit') (previously fire-and-forget) and writes the same shape with codex+openai as the would-be holder. CheckoutPollService.start() ensures the directory exists, drains any stale records from a prior extension lifetime, and registers fs.watch on the directory. processFile reads→deletes→parses→dispatches; deletion is unconditional after read so malformed records can't pin the directory. handleConflict shows a three-action non-blocking information-message (Poll for release / Force override / Dismiss); in-flight de-dup via a Set<string> keyed on pollKey(record) (`<slug>::<would-be engine>+<would-be provider>`) short-circuits duplicate sentinels from the same orchestrator on the same set (e.g., Codex watcher firing repeatedly on config.toml saves). beginPolling registers fs.watch on the held set's session-state.json with 5s debounce; tryRetry re-reads the file, applies the H4 identity gate (isSlotFreeForHolder = orchestrator block null OR (engine, provider) matches would-be holder), spawns start_session WITHOUT --force on slot-free, returns to waiting on writer refusal (concurrent third-party claim mid-retry stays in the polling state). An immediate tryRetry runs once at beginPolling so the slot-already-free case resolves without waiting for the next state-file change. forceOverride spawns start_session WITH --force; the writer's existing _log_force_override path appends the audit line to ~/.dabbler/orchestrator-writer.log (unchanged). Timeout uses new dabblerSessionSets.checkoutPollTimeoutMinutes setting (default 30, range 1..1440, read on each beginPolling so a setting change takes effect without restart). On timeout, surfaces a one-time toast pointing at the 'Dabbler: Release Check-Out' Command Palette action. extension.ts wires the service via safeRegister with a pythonPathResolver mirroring the resolution chain in checkOutOrchestrator.ts / configWatcher.ts and a timeoutMinutesResolver that defensively clamps to [1, 1440]. Tests: Layer-2 (src/test/suite/checkoutPollService.test.ts, 25 tests across 8 suites — parseConflictRecord 7, isSlotFreeForHolder H4 identity 5, pollKey 2, handleConflict dispatch incl. in-flight de-dup 3, beginPolling state machine 4, processFile sentinel ingest 2, conflictDirPath anchor, prompt-action constants) all pass via npm run test:unit (446 pass + 2 pre-existing failures unchanged from S2/S3/S4). Layer-3 (src/test/playwright/checkout-polling.spec.ts) — 1 passing scenario (sentinel consumed on activation, runtime 12.6s on Windows + Playwright _electron.launch with HOME/USERPROFILE override for hermetic conflict-dir routing) + 1 skipped with FIXME (the full polls-then-attaches happy path defers per the same notification-button brittleness S4's release-checkout palette scenario hit; covered exhaustively at Layer 2). Existing S4 Playwright suite: still 4 passing + 2 skipped (verified — no regression from extension.ts wiring change). npx tsc --noEmit clean across the extension. Round A verification via gemini-pro: VERIFIED with no must-fix issues (cost $0.032; bundle 78,491 chars covering CheckoutPollService.ts, the two refactored invokers, extension.ts excerpt, package.json excerpt, both test files, and three ground-truth references — proposal-addendum §9 H3+H4 verdicts, spec.md Session 5, start_session.py refusal-message body). Cumulative routed spend Set 033 ≈$0.165 of $1.25 NTE. One in-session debug iteration noted in the Layer-3 disposition (npx playwright test invoked directly doesn't run the compile step that npm run test:playwright chains; ran npm run compile manually before retrying — passed deterministically thereafter; documented in inline comments).
files_changed: tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/codex/configWatcher.ts (capture exit code; emit conflict sentinel on exit 4)

reason.specifics: Session 6 is the final session of Set 033: close_session cross-tier check-in (clear orchestrator block on both Full AND Lightweight tiers per the mid-Set-029-S6 operator clarification 'Lightweight doesn't excuse skipping the lock'); idempotence — close on already-null block is a successful no-op; unit tests in ai_router/tests/test_close_session.py; docs updates to docs/session-state-schema.md (check-out / check-in section), ai_router/docs/close-out.md (Section 2 + Section 5 stranded-checkout recovery path), docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md (within-set sequential invariant codified); change-log.md aggregation for Set 033; cross-repo CLAUDE.md notification text in docs/cross-repo-checkout-notice.md (one-time copy source for dabbler-platform, dabbler-access-harvester, dabbler-homehealthcare-accessdb); PyPI release of dabbler-ai-router; Marketplace publish of the extension. High-effort holds: cross-tier writer change + docs surface across three canonical files + dual-registry release. Round B may be needed given the cross-tier writer touch — budget for it ($0.10-$0.30 forecast). The S2 multi-in-progress accordion-body bug + the Set 034/035 audit-then-spec follow-up surfaced in S4 remain out of S6's scope.
